figures.caption: Fig. 1: (a) We introduce Gaze4HRI, an extensive benchmark which includes high-quality gaze recordings for 50+ subjects, 3,000+ videos, 600,000+ frames for an HRI setting. (b) Using our dataset, we evaluated the performance of five state-ofthe-art gaze estimation architectures for HRI settings.

TABLE VII: Exp. 4.2. Mutual-Gaze Setup: Error (◦) (subjectlevel mean ± SD) for each condition (H: Horizontal, V: Vertical). βpitch & βyaw: slope of change in error (◦) per change of absolute pitch & yaw eccentricity (◦) (as subjectlevel mean ± SD), as aggregated across all four conditions.
Fig. 4: The four setups used in our analysis.
Fig. 5: Gaze targets on the shared table, for the objectcentered setup. The black and red circles mark targets for head-pose free and head-pose fixed experiments respectively.
